value.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 3** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> src/pages/customization/Customization.jsx (1)
> > `3-8`: _🎯 Functional Correctness_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_ > > **`customizeData` needs to come from real menu data, not an empty array.** > `BaseSelector` maps over `meals` directly, so `const customizeData = [];` leaves the "Choose Your Base" step blank. Wire this to the fetched menu data instead. > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In `@src/pages/customization/Customization.jsx` around lines 3 - 8, The Customize > page is using an empty custom data source, so the base selection UI has nothing > to render. Update Customization.jsx so customizeData is populated from the > fetched menu data instead of being a blank array, and pass that real data into > the flow used by BaseSelector/CustomizeHero so the “Choose Your Base” step has > meals to map over. > ``` > >
> > > >
> >
🧹 Nitpick comments (5)
tests/kitchen-integration.spec.js (2)
`10-10`: _📐 Maintainability & Code Quality_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _⚡ Quick win_ **Use relative paths instead of duplicating the configured `baseURL`.** `playwright.config.js` already sets `baseURL: 'http://localhost:5173'`. Hardcoding absolute URLs here duplicates that config and makes the test brittle to host/port changes across environments.
♻️ Example ```diff - await page.goto('http://localhost:5173/auth/login'); + await page.goto('/auth/login'); ```
Also applies to: 24-24, 29-29, 54-54
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@tests/kitchen-integration.spec.js` at line 10, The Playwright navigation calls in this spec are hardcoding the configured base URL instead of using relative paths. Update the affected page.goto usages in the kitchen integration test to navigate with relative routes, and keep relying on the baseURL from playwright.config.js so the test remains portable across environments. ```
--- `13-14`: _🔒 Security & Privacy_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _⚡ Quick win_ **Avoid hardcoding test credentials in the spec file.** `admin@revive.com` / `admin123` are committed directly in source. Prefer sourcing from environment variables or a test fixtures/secrets file so credentials aren't hardcoded and can be rotated without code changes. Also applies to: 32-32
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@tests/kitchen-integration.spec.js` around lines 13 - 14, The kitchen integration test is hardcoding login credentials in the spec, so update the login flow in the affected test block to read the email and password from environment variables or a test fixture/secrets source instead of embedding admin@revive.com and admin123 directly. Keep the existing page.fill calls, but replace the literal values with configurable test inputs and apply the same change to the other hardcoded credential usage in this spec so credentials can be rotated without code changes. ```
test-results/.last-run.json (1)
`1-4`: _📐 Maintainability & Code Quality_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _⚡ Quick win_ **Don't commit generated Playwright test-result artifacts.** `test-results/.last-run.json` is auto-generated by Playwright after each run and will quickly go stale relative to actual CI results if committed. Add `test-results/` to `.gitignore` instead of tracking it.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@test-results/.last-run.json` around lines 1 - 4, The committed Playwright artifact in test-results/.last-run.json should be removed from source control and ignored going forward. Delete the tracked test-results file(s) and update .gitignore to exclude the entire test-results/ directory so auto-generated Playwright output is not committed again. ```
src/services/api.js (1)
`34-35`: _📐 Maintainability & Code Quality_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _⚡ Quick win_ **Duplicated dynamic import of the auth store in both interceptors.** The same `const useAuthStore = (await import("../store/authStore")).default;` line is repeated in the request and response interceptors. Consider extracting a small memoized helper (e.g., `let authStoreModule; async function getAuthStore() { ... }`) to avoid the duplication and the repeated `import()` promise overhead on every request/response. Also applies to: 49-49
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@src/services/api.js` around lines 34 - 35, The auth store dynamic import is duplicated in both the request and response interceptors, so extract the repeated import in api.js into a small memoized helper such as getAuthStore() and use it from both interceptor callbacks. Keep the helper near the interceptor setup, cache the imported module in a module-scoped variable, and replace the inline import in the request/response paths with calls to that helper. ```
src/tests/services/api.test.js (1)
`124-146`: _🎯 Functional Correctness_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _⚡ Quick win_ **Test's mock JWT payload doesn't exercise base64url edge cases.** `btoa(JSON.stringify({ exp: futureExp }))` only encodes digits/braces/quotes, so the resulting base64 string won't contain `-`/`_`. Real JWTs are base64url-encoded and commonly contain these characters, which `atob()` cannot decode (see comment on `src/services/api.js` lines 80-95). Consider adding a payload with additional claims (e.g., a `sub` string with mixed content) likely to include `+`/`/` in standard base64 — or better, testing with characters that map to `-`/`_` in base64url — so this test would actually catch the decode bug.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@src/tests/services/api.test.js` around lines 124 - 146, The JWT decoding test currently uses a payload that only contains an exp value, so it doesn’t cover base64url characters and won’t catch the decode issue in api.get or the JWT handling path that sets expiresAt. Update the mock token in this test to include additional claims in the payload (for example via the mockPayload/mockJwt setup) so the encoded segment exercises base64url-safe characters like - and _, and ensure the assertion still verifies useAuthStore.getState().expiresAt is derived correctly from the decoded exp value. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@src/services/api.js`: - Around line 9-14: The getBaseURL helper returns undefined for the VITE_API_URL_DEV and VITE_API_URL_PROD branches when those env vars are missing, so update this logic to use an explicit fallback or fail fast with a clear error instead of silently falling back to relative axios requests. Adjust the getBaseURL function so the dev/prod cases behave consistently with the local branch, and keep the base URL resolution centralized in this helper. - Around line 80-95: The refresh-token handling in api.js needs two fixes: decode the JWT payload as base64url instead of plain atob(token.split('.')[1]) so exp is read correctly from normal JWTs, and guard res.data.token before using it. Update the token parsing logic in the refresh flow to convert the URL-safe segment before JSON parsing, and only call setAccessToken plus resolve refreshQueue when a valid token exists; otherwise fail the refresh path cleanly. In `@tests/kitchen-integration.spec.js`: - Around line 8-20: The login flow in test.beforeEach is swallowing failures, so the suite keeps running unauthenticated and hides the real root cause. Update the beforeEach in kitchen-integration.spec.js to let the page.fill/page.click/page.waitForURL failure fail the test instead of catching it, or rethrow after logging so failures stop immediately. Keep the login setup centered around the existing beforeEach hook and the page.goto/page.waitForURL flow so downstream tests only run after a successful login. --- Outside diff comments: In `@src/pages/customization/Customization.jsx`: - Around line 3-8: The Customize page is using an empty custom data source, so the base selection UI has nothing to render. Update Customization.jsx so customizeData is populated from the fetched menu data instead of being a blank array, and pass that real data into the flow used by BaseSelector/CustomizeHero so the “Choose Your Base” step has meals to map over. --- Nitpick comments: In `@src/services/api.js`: - Around line 34-35: The auth store dynamic import is duplicated in both the request and response interceptors, so extract the repeated import in api.js into a small memoized helper such as getAuthStore() and use it from both interceptor callbacks. Keep the helper near the interceptor setup, cache the imported module in a module-scoped variable, and replace the inline import in the request/response paths with calls to that helper. In `@src/tests/services/api.test.js`: - Around line 124-146: The JWT decoding test currently uses a payload that only contains an exp value, so it doesn’t cover base64url characters and won’t catch the decode issue in api.get or the JWT handling path that sets expiresAt. Update the mock token in this test to include additional claims in the payload (for example via the mockPayload/mockJwt setup) so the encoded segment exercises base64url-safe characters like - and _, and ensure the assertion still verifies useAuthStore.getState().expiresAt is derived correctly from the decoded exp value. In `@test-results/.last-run.json`: - Around line 1-4: The committed Playwright artifact in test-results/.last-run.json should be removed from source control and ignored going forward. Delete the tracked test-results file(s) and update .gitignore to exclude the entire test-results/ directory so auto-generated Playwright output is not committed again. In `@tests/kitchen-integration.spec.js`: - Line 10: The Playwright navigation calls in this spec are hardcoding the configured base URL instead of using relative paths. Update the affected page.goto usages in the kitchen integration test to navigate with relative routes, and keep relying on the baseURL from playwright.config.js so the test remains portable across environments. - Around line 13-14: The kitchen integration test is hardcoding login credentials in the spec, so update the login flow in the affected test block to read the email and password from environment variables or a test fixture/secrets source instead of embedding admin@revive.com and admin123 directly. Keep the existing page.fill calls, but replace the literal values with configurable test inputs and apply the same change to the other hardcoded credential usage in this spec so credentials can be rotated without code changes. ```
🪄 Autofix (Beta) Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] Create a new PR with the fixes
---
